subtitle: Surgical Oncology Edition
feed_desc: Adequate LND improves OS in esophageal cancer pCR after neoadjuvant HR 0.70 meta-analysis (Annals of Surgical Oncology), Pulmonary mets after PDAC surgery mOS 30 mo vs liver 13 mo prognostic hierarchy (Annals of Surgical Oncology), Perioperative chemo completion HR 0.20 for 1-year OS after total gastrectomy (World J Surg Oncol), EOCRC vs LOCRC 5-year survival in 92K patients with distinct prognostic profiles (J Gastroenterol Hepatol), SLC1A3 aspartate transporter promotes CRC via MDM2-p53 and M2 macrophage polarization (Cancer Science)

papers.title: Adequate Lymph Node Dissection Improves Overall Survival in Esophageal Cancer Complete Responders After Neoadjuvant Therapy: A Meta-Analysis
papers.authors: Zhou X, Chen D, Song P, Zheng Y, et al.
papers.summary: Meta-analysis of 8 studies involving 2,578 patients with pathologic complete response (pCR) after neoadjuvant therapy for esophageal cancer. Adequate lymph node dissection (LND) was significantly associated with improved overall survival (HR 0.70; 95% CI 0.58-0.84, p<0.001). No additional benefit was observed when harvested lymph nodes exceeded 40. Subgroup analyses revealed that adequate LND was significantly associated with superior OS in Western and adenocarcinoma-predominant populations but not in Eastern populations or squamous cell carcinoma, suggesting histology-tailored lymphadenectomy strategies.
papers.post_angle: Even after pCR, lymph node dissection matters in esophageal cancer — but context is everything. Adenocarcinoma benefits, SCC doesn't. Personalized surgery. #Esophageal #SurgOnc #GIOnc #PrecisionMedicine

papers.title: Pulmonary Metastases After PDAC Resection Show Superior Survival: A Prognostic Hierarchy of Recurrence Patterns
papers.authors: D'Souza M, Andree M, Feili A, Sadr-Azodi O, Holmberg M.
papers.summary: Analysis of 343 resected PDAC patients from Karolinska University Hospital. 85% recurred within 3 years, with a clear prognostic hierarchy: pulmonary metastases mOS 30 months (95% CI 27-35), other sites 20 months (95% CI 19-26), and liver 13 months (95% CI 12-16), p<0.001. Mortality risk compared to no recurrence was 7-fold for lung, 12-fold for other, and 23-fold for liver. These findings support consideration of targeted treatment strategies — including potentially aggressive local approaches — for the lung recurrence subgroup.
papers.post_angle: Not all PDAC recurrences are equal. Lung mets: mOS 30 mo. Liver mets: 13 mo. A 23-fold vs 7-fold mortality gap. This prognostic hierarchy should guide treatment intensity. #PDAC #PancreaticCancer #SurgOnc #GIOnc

papers.title: Completion of Perioperative Chemotherapy Dramatically Improves 1-Year Survival After Total Gastrectomy for Gastric Cancer
papers.authors: Aldarwish M, Hoelzen JP, El-Sourani N, et al.
papers.summary: Retrospective analysis of 145 patients undergoing total gastrectomy for gastric cancer (2012-2023). Completion of the perioperative chemotherapy sequence was independently associated with markedly improved 1-year OS compared to neoadjuvant alone (HR 0.20; 95% CI 0.08-0.50, p=0.001), remaining significant in 60-day landmark analysis. Critically, 55.3% of patients who started neoadjuvant chemotherapy did not proceed to the adjuvant phase, primarily due to patient refusal or medical contraindications. Poorer tumor regression grade showed a prognostic trend (HR 1.60, p=0.059).
papers.post_angle: HR 0.20 — completing perioperative chemo after gastrectomy is an 80% mortality reduction at 1 year. Yet 55% of patients never finish. This is a quality gap we can close. #GastricCancer #SurgOnc #GIOnc #QualityOfCare

papers.title: Early-Onset vs Later-Onset Colorectal Cancer: Distinct Survival Profiles in 92,365 Patients From Taiwan
papers.authors: Kuan TC, Wu CY, Chen CC, Giovannucci E, Liu JJ.
papers.summary: Nationwide study of 92,365 CRC patients from the Taiwan Cancer Registry (2002-2015). EOCRC patients (<50 years) presented with more advanced staging but demonstrated slightly better 5-year CRC-specific survival than LOCRC after adjusting for sex and tumor characteristics — across all CRC sites. EOCRC patients had more females, more current alcohol/cigarette users, and less right-sided disease. Notably, female sex and treatment delay <30 days were associated with improved survival in LOCRC but not EOCRC, suggesting distinct biology. Underweight BMI was a risk factor only in LOCRC.
papers.post_angle: 92K patients confirm: early-onset CRC is a different disease. More advanced at diagnosis yet better cancer-specific survival. The biology differs — we need age-specific screening & treatment strategies. #EOCRC #CRC #GIOnc

papers.title: Aspartate Transporter SLC1A3 Drives Colorectal Cancer Through MDM2-p53 Pathway and M2 Macrophage Polarization
papers.authors: Deng C, Zhou Y, Song S, Liu H, et al.
papers.summary: SLC1A3, the primary aspartate transporter, is significantly overexpressed in CRC and associated with poor prognosis. This study reveals a dual mechanism: SLC1A3 enhances CRC cell proliferation, invasion, and migration through the DAG/PKC/MDM2 signaling axis (suppressing p53), while simultaneously inducing immunosuppressive M2 macrophage polarization via upregulation of IL17c and CSF2. This dual tumor-intrinsic and immune-modulatory role makes SLC1A3 a promising therapeutic target that could simultaneously address tumor growth and immune evasion.
papers.post_angle: One transporter, two mechanisms: SLC1A3 drives CRC growth via MDM2-p53 AND reshapes the immune microenvironment via M2 macrophage polarization. A dual-target opportunity. #CRC #Immunology #PrecisionMedicine #GIOnc

additional.title: Emerging and Established Targets in CRC: Translating Biology Into Therapeutics
additional.source_line: ASCO Educational Book — Comprehensive review of KRAS G12C combos, BRAF strategies, ADCs, bispecific antibodies, DDR inhibitors, and TME-modulating agents reshaping precision CRC oncology (Lenz HJ et al.)

additional.title: Updated Epidemiology of GI Cancers in the Asia-Pacific Region
additional.source_line: J Gastroenterol Hepatol — GBD 2021 + GLOBOCAN 2022 analysis: CRC ranks highest ASIR across Asia-Pacific; Mongolia disproportionately burdened by esophageal, gastric, and liver cancers

additional.title: METTL3-m6A-MALAT1-NF-kB Regulatory Axis in Colorectal Cancer Progression
additional.source_line: J Translational Medicine — METTL3 stabilizes MALAT1 via m6A modification, activating NF-kB through IkBa degradation and p65 nuclear translocation; METTL3 inhibitor suppresses xenograft growth

additional.title: Histotripsy Dose Optimization in Preclinical HCC Model
additional.source_line: Scientific Reports — Histotripsy dose impacts cellular damage and treatment outcomes in HCC; dose optimization enables abscopal immune effects

additional.title: Rubimaillin as Natural Product KRAS G12D Inhibitor in Pancreatic Cancer
additional.source_line: Molecular Diversity — TCM-derived rubimaillin shows IC50 1.10 uM in MIA PaCa-2 cells, reduces KRAS protein, modulates p53/BCL2, inhibits migration in PDAC cell lines

x_thread.label: Post 1 — Hook
x_thread.text:
🔪 GI Oncology Digest — April 27, 2026
Surgical Oncology Edition

Today's highlights:
• Even after pCR, lymphadenectomy matters in esophageal cancer — but NOT for SCC
• Not all PDAC recurrences are equal: lung mets mOS 30 mo vs liver 13 mo
• Finishing perioperative chemo = 80% mortality reduction in gastric cancer
• 92K-patient study redefines EOCRC as a distinct disease

A thread 🧵👇

#GIOnc #SurgOnc #EsophagealCancer #PDAC #GastricCancer #EOCRC

x_thread.label: Post 2 — #1 LND Esophageal pCR
x_thread.text:
🔥 #1 Lymph Node Dissection in Esophageal Cancer pCR
(Annals of Surgical Oncology)

• Meta-analysis: 2,578 pCR patients across 8 studies
• Adequate LND → HR 0.70 for OS (p<0.001)
• No extra benefit beyond 40 lymph nodes
• Key caveat: benefit seen in adenocarcinoma + Western patients
• No significant OS benefit in SCC or Eastern populations

This is why biology trumps one-size-fits-all surgery. Histology should guide the extent of lymphadenectomy — even after complete pathologic response.

#Esophageal #SurgOnc #GIOnc #PrecisionSurgery

x_thread.label: Post 3 — #2 PDAC Recurrence Hierarchy
x_thread.text:
📊 #2 PDAC Recurrence Prognostic Hierarchy
(Annals of Surgical Oncology)

• 343 resected PDAC patients, 85% recurred within 3 years
• Lung mets: mOS 30 mo
• Other sites: mOS 20 mo
• Liver mets: mOS 13 mo
• Mortality risk: lung 7x, other 12x, liver 23x vs no recurrence

This changes the conversation about recurrent PDAC. Lung-only recurrence patients deserve a different treatment paradigm — potentially even oligometastatic approaches.

#PDAC #PancreaticCancer #SurgOnc #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 4 — #3 Perioperative Chemo Gastric
x_thread.text:
🎯 #3 Completing Perioperative Chemo After Gastrectomy
(World J Surg Oncol)

• 145 total gastrectomy patients
• Completing perioperative chemo vs neoadjuvant alone: HR 0.20 (p=0.001)
• That's an 80% reduction in 1-year mortality
• BUT 55% of patients never finished the adjuvant phase
• Main barriers: patient refusal + medical contraindications

The biggest gap in gastric cancer outcomes isn't drug discovery — it's treatment completion. We must do better at supporting patients through the full course.

#GastricCancer #SurgOnc #QualityOfCare #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 5 — #4 EOCRC Taiwan
x_thread.text:
🌍 #4 Early-Onset CRC: A Distinct Disease in 92K Patients
(J Gastroenterol Hepatol)

• Taiwan Cancer Registry: 92,365 CRC patients (2002-2015)
• EOCRC (<50y): more advanced staging BUT better CRC-specific survival
• Female sex + treatment delay protected LOCRC but NOT EOCRC
• Underweight BMI = risk factor only in LOCRC
• EOCRC: more left-sided, more females, more alcohol/tobacco use

With Giovannucci as co-author, this massive dataset confirms EOCRC has fundamentally different biology. Age-specific treatment strategies are overdue.

#EOCRC #CRC #Epidemiology #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 6 — #5 SLC1A3 CRC
x_thread.text:
🧪 #5 SLC1A3: One Transporter, Two Cancer Mechanisms
(Cancer Science)

• Aspartate transporter SLC1A3 overexpressed in CRC = poor prognosis
• Mechanism 1: DAG/PKC/MDM2 axis → p53 suppression → proliferation
• Mechanism 2: IL17c/CSF2 → M2 macrophage polarization → immune evasion
• Enhances proliferation, invasion, migration in cells + organoids

A metabolic transporter that simultaneously drives tumor growth AND immune escape? That's a dual-target opportunity worth pursuing.

#CRC #Immunology #MetabolicOncology #GIOnc

x_thread.text:

• Emerging CRC targets: KRAS combos, BRAF strategies, ADCs, bispecifics — ASCO Ed Book review by Lenz et al.
• GI cancer epidemiology update: Asia-Pacific GBD 2021 + GLOBOCAN 2022 analysis
• METTL3-m6A-MALAT1 axis drives CRC via NF-kB pathway (J Transl Med)
• Rubimaillin: natural product KRAS G12D inhibitor IC50 1.1 uM in PDAC cells
